markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar's confirmation of Grand Theft Auto VI's November 19, 2026 release date, reaffirmed by Take-Two Interactive in May 2026 earnings commentary without further delays, has fixed a clear timeline that shapes trader assessments of preceding events. This extended window through late fall allows markets to price high-probability outcomes such as geopolitical resolutions like a Russia-Ukraine ceasefire or music releases including Drake's Iceman album, based on their alignment with the roughly five-month horizon. Industry dynamics amplify focus on Rockstar's typical promotional cadence, including potential GTA Online summer DLC drops around July and any late-cycle trailer activity, while the game's anticipated cultural dominance influences ancillary pop culture timing. Traders weigh these catalysts against historical patterns of major album cycles and news developments that could resolve before launch.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent mistrials and procedural setbacks in Harvey Weinstein’s New York proceedings, including a deadlocked jury in May 2026 on the remaining rape charge after a mixed 2025 verdict, have reinforced trader consensus around No Prison Time at 80.8% implied probability. Weinstein, already facing a 16-year California sentence for rape and sexual assault that runs consecutively to any New York outcome, remains at Rikers amid health concerns and plea discussions that could limit additional time served. His age, documented medical conditions, and repeated appeals have shifted focus toward concurrent sentencing or resolution without further penalties, while lower-probability brackets like 20-30 years reflect slim chances of stacked convictions. Upcoming plea negotiations or retrial scheduling represent key swing factors that could adjust these aggregated trader assessments.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Carolina Hurricanes hold an 79.5% implied probability in the 2026 Stanley Cup futures market primarily because they lead the best-of-seven Final 3-2 against the Vegas Golden Knights, with Game 6 set for June 14 in Las Vegas. Carolina reached the Final after a dominant playoff run that included a 4-1 Eastern Conference Final win over Montreal, building on their status as the Metropolitan Division and Eastern Conference regular-season leaders with 113 points. Vegas advanced by sweeping the top-seeded Colorado Avalanche in the West but has trailed in the series after dropping three of five games. Recent results, including Carolina’s Game 5 victory, have reinforced trader consensus around the Hurricanes’ depth, possession advantages, and experience in closing out series, while Vegas retains realistic comeback potential at home.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain and France lead the World Cup winner market due to their proven depth, recent major-tournament pedigree, and balanced squads featuring elite attackers and midfield control suited to the expanded 48-team format. Spain’s Euro 2024 title and Olympic success underpin its narrow edge, though fitness questions around Lamine Yamal and others create uncertainty. France’s repeated final appearances and attacking options keep it within striking distance. Portugal, England, Brazil, and defending champion Argentina sit tightly clustered behind them because each brings star quality, strong qualifying records, and favorable group-stage positioning, while emerging sides like Norway add further variance. Early group results, including co-host momentum, have produced only modest odds shifts so far, underscoring how evenly matched the contenders remain entering the knockout phase.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

Recent diplomatic activity, including a May 2026 summit with U.S. President Trump and other high-level engagements, underscores his active role without signs of internal challenges or health-related constraints. The 21st Party Congress scheduled for late 2027 is widely viewed as the venue for a fourth term rather than a transition, reinforcing consensus around continuity through the end of 2026.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads the Democratic presidential nomination market at 23.3% because his high national profile as California governor, combined with aggressive redistricting moves and recent populist positioning on AI regulation and consumer protections, has boosted his visibility among party activists and donors. eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads the Democratic presidential nomination market at 23.3% because his high national profile as California governor, combined with aggressive redistricting moves and recent populist positioning on AI regulation and consumer protections, has boosted his visibility among party activists and donors. A fragmented field keeps Jon Ossoff, Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, and Kamala Harris in single digits, reflecting divided preferences between Senate experience, progressive messaging, and prior national ticket exposure. Key differentiators include governors’ executive records versus legislators’ ideological lanes, with upcoming midterms offering early tests of grassroots support. Consolidation behind one contender would likely require strong early primary polling, major endorsements, or clear fundraising edges that shift trader consensus away from the current spread.
